system_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer the user's question helpfully and specifically. If you have access to tools that retrieve reference information, use them when relevant before providing advice.
turn1_user_message: Quick travel question. I'm a US passport holder heading to Vietnam in two weeks for a 12-day trip — Hanoi and Ha Long Bay, just tourism. Do I need a visa for Vietnam? My passport is valid for another 3 years.
turn2_user_message: Perfect, thanks. Can you write me a quick trip-prep checklist for the departure airport — what documents to bring, what to expect at check-in and at Hanoi immigration, and anything I should print? I travel light and want to make sure I'm not missing anything.
response_turn1:
# Vietnam Visa for US Passport Holders

## Good News — You Likely Don't Need a Visa!

As of **August 2023**, Vietnam extended its visa exemption for US citizens (and many other nationalities) to **45 days**, up from the previous 15 days.

### Your situation checks out:
- ✅ **12 days** — well within the 45-day limit
- ✅ **US passport** — eligible for exemption
- ✅ **Tourism** — qualifying purpose
- ✅ **3 years validity** — meets the requirement (passport must typically be valid **at least 6 months** beyond entry)

---

## What You Still Need to Do

- **No advance visa application required** — you get the exemption stamp on arrival
- **Have your return/onward ticket** ready to show immigration
- **Proof of accommodation** can also be helpful to have

---

## A Few Things to Double-Check

- **Confirm current rules** haven't changed closer to your trip at the official Vietnamese embassy site or [travel.state.gov](https://travel.state.gov)
- The exemption typically allows **one entry** — if you're crossing into another country mid-trip and returning, that could complicate things (doesn't sound like your plan though)

---

Your trip sounds straightforward. Ha Long Bay is fantastic — enjoy it!
response_turn2:
# Vietnam Trip Departure Checklist

## Documents to Bring
- ✅ **US Passport** (valid 3+ years — you're good)
- ✅ **Return/onward flight ticket** — have it accessible (printed or on phone)
- ✅ **Hotel/accommodation confirmations** for all stays
- ✅ **Travel insurance** (not required but strongly recommended — have policy number handy)
- ✅ **Any booking confirmations** for Ha Long Bay tours

---

## What to Print (or Have Offline on Your Phone)
- Return flight confirmation
- Hotel confirmations (Hanoi + Ha Long Bay)
- Travel insurance policy
- Emergency contacts / embassy number
- *Optional but useful:* Copy of your passport photo page

> **Tip:** Vietnam immigration can have spotty WiFi — having these **offline or printed** saves stress.

---

## At Check-In (US Departure Airport)
- Airline will verify your passport validity
- They may ask about your visa — just say **"visa exemption, 45 days"**
- Check your **baggage allowance** in advance — budget airlines on connecting flights sometimes differ
- Arrive **3 hours early** for international departures

---

## At Hanoi (Noi Bai) Immigration
- Follow signs for **"Foreigners"** passport control
- Hand over your **passport** — no visa paperwork needed
- Officer may ask:
  - Purpose of visit → **"Tourism"**
  - How long → **"12 days"**
  - Where staying → have hotel name ready
- Get your **entry stamp** — you're done
- Customs form may or may not be required (Vietnam removed the arrival card for most travelers — confirm this is still current closer to your trip)

---

## Quick Practical Tips
- **Download offline maps** (Google Maps or Maps.me) for Hanoi before you fly
- **Grab local currency (VND)** at airport ATMs on arrival — better rates than US airport exchanges
- **SIM card** available cheaply at Noi Bai airport if you want local data
- Let your **bank/credit card** know you're traveling

---

You're traveling light and your trip is straightforward — this should cover everything. Have a great trip!
